---
date: 2026/06/19
date-format: long
date-modified: last-modified
---

# Methods

![](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-WIP-red) This project is a work in progress and the results should be read as preliminary.

This project analyses **co-occurrence patterns of plant taxa across space, time, and biological resolution** using fossil pollen records, modern vegetation records, co-located climate data, site coordinates, and plant functional traits.
The central aim is to quantify how much of the observed co-occurrence signal is explained by shared abiotic responses, spatial or spatiotemporal structure, and residual species-species association signals.

The project is part of the [BIODYNAMICS](https://bit.ly/BIODYNAMICS) initiative.

## Data Sources

### VegVault

All primary vegetation, climate, coordinate, and trait inputs are sourced from [VegVault](https://bit.ly/VegVault), a harmonised SQLite database that combines fossil pollen archives, modern vegetation datasets, co-registered climate reconstructions, and plant functional traits.
Using a single harmonised data source keeps site identifiers, sample ages, coordinates, taxonomy, and climate values consistent across the paleo, modern, and trait workflows.
For each analysis, records are extracted for the spatial window, temporal range, data source, and predictor set defined by the active configuration in `config.yml`.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details - data extraction

The function [`extract_data_from_vegvault()`](Documentation/Functions/extract_data_from_vegvault.qmd) queries a local VegVault SQLite database and returns a unified tidy object containing community and abiotic records for the requested analysis.
Coordinates are extracted with [`get_coords()`](Documentation/Functions/get_coords.qmd), which records one coordinate pair per dataset rather than one coordinate pair per sample.
Database presence and accessibility are checked at session startup with [`check_presence_of_vegvault()`](Documentation/Functions/check_presence_of_vegvault.qmd).

**Configuration-controlled extraction filters**:

- Spatial window: `x_lim` and `y_lim`
- Temporal window: `age_lim`
- Dataset types: `sel_dataset_type`
- Abiotic variables: `sel_abiotic_var_name`
- Target store root: `target_store`

**Current data families**:

| Data family | Example configurations |
|---|---|
| Paleo temporal | `project_paleo_temporal_europe`, `project_paleo_temporal_america`, `project_paleo_temporal_asia` |
| Paleo spatial | `project_paleo_spatial_continental`, `project_paleo_spatial_regional`, `project_paleo_spatial_local` |
| Modern spatial | `project_modern_spatial_continental`, `project_modern_spatial_regional`, `project_modern_spatial_local` |
| Traits reference | `project_traits_reference` |
| Development checks | `project_cz_paleo`, `project_cz_modern` |
:::

### Climate Variables

Climate predictors represent the shared abiotic response component of the joint species distribution models.
Paleo analyses use CHELSA-TraCE21k bioclimatic variables co-registered with the pollen records in VegVault.
The broad candidate set includes variables describing mean thermal energy, temperature seasonality and extremes, annual precipitation, and seasonal precipitation.

To prevent redundant predictors from inflating coefficient uncertainty, the pipeline screens candidate climate variables before model fitting.
The current workflow removes invariant predictors, evaluates collinearity, and retains a non-redundant predictor subset for the active dataset.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details - abiotic processing

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_abiotic_extract.R`

| Target | Function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `data_abiotic_ages` | [`add_age_to_samples()`](Documentation/Functions/add_age_to_samples.qmd) | Abiotic records with sample age attached |
| `data_abiotic_selected` | [`select_non_collinear_predictors()`](Documentation/Functions/select_non_collinear_predictors.qmd) | Non-redundant predictor subset |
| `data_abiotic_interpolated` | [`interpolate_data()`](Documentation/Functions/interpolate_data.qmd) | Climate records interpolated to the configured time grid where needed |
| `data_abiotic_scaled_list` | [`scale_abiotic_for_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/scale_abiotic_for_fit.qmd) | Centred and scaled predictors plus scaling attributes |

Predictor screening and scaling are performed inside each target store, so each spatial unit, time-slice workflow, and data source can have its own model-ready predictor matrix.
:::

### Plant Functional Traits

Plant traits are used to build functional-type classifications that can be compared with genus- and family-level community matrices.
The traits workflow extracts raw trait values from VegVault, flags problematic values, applies curated corrections, aggregates validated trait values to taxa, and clusters taxa into functional types.
Functional types are generated as reference classifications and then consumed by the spatial-resolution pipelines.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details - trait processing

**Pipeline**: `pipeline_traits_reference.R`

| Stage | Pipe segment or function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Trait extraction | `pipe_segment_traits_extract.R` | Extract trait domains and raw values from VegVault |
| Trait QC | `pipe_segment_traits_qc.R` | Flag impossible values and outliers for review |
| Classification | `pipe_segment_traits_classification.R` | Resolve trait taxa against the project taxonomy |
| Corrected QC | `pipe_segment_traits_qc_classified.R` | Apply reviewed corrections after classification |
| Trait table | `pipe_segment_traits_table.R` | Build taxon-by-trait summaries |
| Functional clustering | `pipe_segment_traits_ft_clustering.R` | Cluster taxa into functional types |

Key helper functions include [`extract_traits_from_vegvault()`](Documentation/Functions/extract_traits_from_vegvault.qmd), [`clean_raw_trait_data()`](Documentation/Functions/clean_raw_trait_data.qmd), [`apply_trait_corrections()`](Documentation/Functions/apply_trait_corrections.qmd), [`aggregate_trait_values()`](Documentation/Functions/aggregate_trait_values.qmd), and [`cluster_functional_types()`](Documentation/Functions/cluster_functional_types.qmd).
:::

## Analysis Axes

The current project structure is broader than the original two-pipeline design.
The analysis now spans paleo and modern data sources, three spatial scales, temporal slices through the late Quaternary, and multiple taxonomic or functional resolutions.

::: {.callout-important}
## Complementary analysis axes

### Temporal - *How do co-occurrence patterns change through time?*

`pipeline_paleo_temporal.R` fits the shared modelling core independently across paleo time slices.
Each branch receives a target age from `tarchetypes::tar_map()`, filters the aligned sample set to that time slice, fits a model, calculates variance decomposition, and summarises temporal trajectories.

**Configurations**: `project_paleo_temporal_europe`, `project_paleo_temporal_america`, `project_paleo_temporal_asia`

### Spatial - *How do co-occurrence patterns vary across geographic scale?*

`pipeline_paleo_spatial_resolution.R` and `pipeline_modern_spatial_resolution.R` run the shared modelling core independently for geographic units defined in `Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv`.
Spatial units are stored separately with `store_suffix`, so each unit has its own target store and progress graph.

**Configurations**: `project_paleo_spatial_continental`, `project_paleo_spatial_regional`, `project_paleo_spatial_local`, `project_modern_spatial_continental`, `project_modern_spatial_regional`, `project_modern_spatial_local`

### Biological resolution - *Do conclusions depend on how taxa are grouped?*

Spatial-resolution pipelines branch over community resolution.
Paleo spatial analyses compare genus, family, and functional-type matrices.
Modern spatial analyses support genus, family, and functional-type variants routed through the current branch labels.

**Resolution branches**: genus, family, and functional type.
:::

The analytical core is shared across these axes.
The main differences are how samples are selected, which community resolution is supplied to the model, and whether the spatial component is purely spatial or spatiotemporal.

## Analysis Pipeline

The diagram below summarises the current shared workflow and its main branch points.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {
  'background': '#0B0F14',
  'primaryColor': '#141B22',
  'primaryTextColor': '#E6EDF3',
  'primaryBorderColor': '#2A3441',
  'lineColor': '#98A6B3',
  'secondaryColor': '#141B22',
  'tertiaryColor': '#141B22',
  'edgeLabelBackground': '#141B22',
  'clusterBkg': '#141B22',
  'titleColor': '#E6EDF3',
  'fontFamily': 'IBM Plex Mono, monospace'
}}}%%
flowchart TD
    A[(VegVault)] --> B["Community<br/>extraction"]
    A --> C["Abiotic<br/>extraction"]
    A --> D["Coordinates"]
    A --> E["Traits"]
    E --> F["Functional-type<br/>classification"]
    B --> G{"Data source"}
    G -- "Paleo" --> H["Paleo community<br/>preparation"]
    G -- "Modern" --> I["Modern QC and<br/>deduplication"]
    H --> J{"Analysis axis"}
    I --> J
    C --> J
    D --> J
    F --> K{"Resolution"}
    J --> K
    K --> L["Sample alignment"]
    L --> M["Model input<br/>preparation"]
    M --> N["MEM predictors"]
    N --> O["Fit jSDM"]
    O --> P["Model evaluation"]
    O --> Q["Variance<br/>decomposition"]
    O --> R["Prediction and<br/>visualisation"]
    L --> S["Temporal network<br/>metrics"]
    style J fill:#D98CFF,color:#0B0F14,stroke:#2A3441
    style K fill:#48C7B8,color:#0B0F14,stroke:#2A3441
    style O fill:#FFB35C,color:#0B0F14,stroke:#2A3441
```

## Pipeline Stages

### 1. VegVault Data Extraction

Vegetation records, climate data, and coordinates are queried from a local VegVault SQLite database for the active configuration.
The extraction produces parallel data objects that remain separate through preprocessing and are only merged at sample alignment and model assembly.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_vegvault_extract.R`

| Target | Function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `data_vegvault_extracted` | [`extract_data_from_vegvault()`](Documentation/Functions/extract_data_from_vegvault.qmd) | Raw community and abiotic records from VegVault |
| `data_coords` | [`get_coords()`](Documentation/Functions/get_coords.qmd) | One coordinate pair per `dataset_name` |
| `check_n_cores` | [`check_min_n_cores()`](Documentation/Functions/check_min_n_cores.qmd) | Guard for minimum available compute resources |

The extraction segment is reused by paleo, modern, and development pipelines.
Target store isolation is controlled by the active configuration and, for spatial workflows, by `store_suffix`.
:::

### 2. Community Data Extraction

Community data are extracted from the VegVault result object and converted into the long-format structure expected by downstream preprocessing.
This stage keeps the raw taxon names and sample metadata available for classification, interpolation, filtering, and audit checks.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_community_extract.R`

| Step | Target or helper | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Community extraction | [`get_community_data()`](Documentation/Functions/get_community_data.qmd) | Extract community records from the VegVault object |
| Long conversion | [`make_community_data_long()`](Documentation/Functions/make_community_data_long.qmd) | Convert community data to tidy long format |
| Age attachment | [`add_age_to_samples()`](Documentation/Functions/add_age_to_samples.qmd) | Attach sample ages before interpolation or filtering |
| Pollen sums | [`get_pollen_sum()`](Documentation/Functions/get_pollen_sum.qmd) | Calculate pollen sums used in proportional filtering |
:::

### 3. Taxonomic Classification

Taxonomic classification resolves source taxon names to a consistent project taxonomy.
The workflow combines automated classification, project-specific auxiliary tables, and missing-taxon reporting so unresolved or manually corrected cases remain explicit.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_taxa_classification.R`

| Target or helper | Description |
|---|---|
| [`get_taxa_classification()`](Documentation/Functions/get_taxa_classification.qmd) | Retrieves taxonomic classifications |
| [`get_aux_classification_table()`](Documentation/Functions/get_aux_classification_table.qmd) | Reads project-specific manual classifications |
| [`combine_classification_tables()`](Documentation/Functions/combine_classification_tables.qmd) | Combines automatic and auxiliary classifications |
| [`get_taxa_without_classification()`](Documentation/Functions/get_taxa_without_classification.qmd) | Identifies unresolved taxa |
| [`append_missing_taxa_to_template()`](Documentation/Functions/append_missing_taxa_to_template.qmd) | Updates missing-taxon templates for review |
| [`check_and_report_missing_taxa()`](Documentation/Functions/check_and_report_missing_taxa.qmd) | Produces explicit missing-classification reports |

This stage supports the later genus, family, and functional-type branches.
:::

### 4. Paleo Community Preparation

Paleo community preparation transforms fossil pollen counts into a filtered, classified, and temporally standardised community table.
The workflow converts counts to proportions, propagates sample ages, extracts age uncertainty, interpolates irregular records to the configured temporal grid, removes non-plant taxa, resolves taxonomic resolution, and applies data-density filters.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_community_prepare_paleo.R`

| Step | Target or function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Proportions | [`make_community_proportions()`](Documentation/Functions/make_community_proportions.qmd) | Converts counts to proportional abundance |
| Age uncertainty | [`extract_age_uncertainty_from_vegvault()`](Documentation/Functions/extract_age_uncertainty_from_vegvault.qmd) | Retrieves age uncertainty information |
| Interpolation index | [`make_community_interpolation_index()`](Documentation/Functions/make_community_interpolation_index.qmd) | Builds interpolation jobs for fossil sequences |
| Interpolation jobs | [`make_community_interpolation_jobs()`](Documentation/Functions/make_community_interpolation_jobs.qmd) | Splits interpolation work into branchable units |
| Dataset interpolation | [`interpolate_community_dataset_from_shared_inputs()`](Documentation/Functions/interpolate_community_dataset_from_shared_inputs.qmd) | Interpolates one dataset from shared inputs |
| Community interpolation | [`interpolate_community_data()`](Documentation/Functions/interpolate_community_data.qmd) | Combines interpolated community records |
| Plantae filter | [`filter_non_plantae_taxa()`](Documentation/Functions/filter_non_plantae_taxa.qmd) | Removes non-plant records |
| Rare-taxon filter | [`filter_rare_taxa()`](Documentation/Functions/filter_rare_taxa.qmd) | Removes low-proportion records |
| Core and sample filters | [`filter_community_by_n_cores()`](Documentation/Functions/filter_community_by_n_cores.qmd), [`filter_by_n_samples()`](Documentation/Functions/filter_by_n_samples.qmd) | Enforce data-density requirements |

Age uncertainty and interpolation are central to the paleo workflow because fossil samples are irregularly spaced and have uncertainty from age-depth modelling.
:::

### 5. Modern Community Preparation

Modern community preparation shares the same broad goal as the paleo workflow but has different quality-control problems.
Modern vegetation data are checked for impossible values, exact duplicates, colocated records, and source-specific aggregation cases before they are filtered and supplied to the shared modelling stack.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_community_prepare_modern.R`

| Step | Target or function | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Quality report | [`make_modern_data_quality_report()`](Documentation/Functions/make_modern_data_quality_report.qmd) | Summarises modern-data quality checks |
| Impossible values | [`check_modern_data_impossible_values()`](Documentation/Functions/check_modern_data_impossible_values.qmd) | Flags invalid ages, coordinates, or abundance values |
| Duplicate sites | [`detect_duplicate_sites()`](Documentation/Functions/detect_duplicate_sites.qmd) | Detects repeated site metadata |
| Duplicate communities | [`detect_duplicate_communities()`](Documentation/Functions/detect_duplicate_communities.qmd) | Detects exact duplicate community records |
| Colocated records | [`detect_colocated_community_records()`](Documentation/Functions/detect_colocated_community_records.qmd) | Finds records sharing coordinates and age |
| Deduplication | [`deduplicate_modern_community_data()`](Documentation/Functions/deduplicate_modern_community_data.qmd) | Removes exact duplicates deterministically |
| Colocated aggregation | [`aggregate_colocated_community_records()`](Documentation/Functions/aggregate_colocated_community_records.qmd) | Aggregates eligible colocated records |
| Source validation | [`validate_community_source()`](Documentation/Functions/validate_community_source.qmd) | Checks community source labels |

Cross-database colocations are tracked rather than silently merged unless the aggregation rules explicitly allow it.
This keeps modern data processing auditable.
:::

### 6. Community Resolution Routing

After shared community preparation, spatial-resolution pipelines branch over biological resolution.
The same input data can be routed to genus-level, family-level, or functional-type community matrices before alignment and model fitting.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Paleo segment**: `pipe_segment_community_by_resolution_paleo.R`

**Modern segment**: `pipe_segment_community_by_resolution_modern.R`

| Resolution | Main operation |
|---|---|
| Genus | Resolve taxa to genus-level community columns |
| Family | Resolve taxa to family-level community columns |
| Functional type | Collapse taxon presences to functional-type groups |

Key helpers include [`classify_taxonomic_resolution()`](Documentation/Functions/classify_taxonomic_resolution.qmd), [`resolve_classification_to_finest_rank()`](Documentation/Functions/resolve_classification_to_finest_rank.qmd), [`classify_to_functional_type()`](Documentation/Functions/classify_to_functional_type.qmd), and [`replace_na_community_data_with_zeros()`](Documentation/Functions/replace_na_community_data_with_zeros.qmd).

Functional-type classifications are generated before the resolution branches need them.
Spatial-resolution pipelines read those classifications with helpers such as [`get_functional_type_classification_path_from_store()`](Documentation/Functions/get_functional_type_classification_path_from_store.qmd) and [`read_functional_type_classification()`](Documentation/Functions/read_functional_type_classification.qmd).
:::

### 7. Data Alignment

Data alignment defines which samples enter the model.
The pipeline retains only sample identifiers that can be matched across community data, abiotic predictors, and coordinates.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Spatial segment**: `pipe_segment_sample_alignment.R`

**Temporal segment**: `pipe_segment_sample_filter_age.R`

| Path | Sample-selection logic |
|---|---|
| Spatial | [`align_sample_ids()`](Documentation/Functions/align_sample_ids.qmd) computes the intersection across community, abiotic, and coordinate records for the whole spatial unit |
| Temporal | The time-slice branch supplies an age value and filters aligned samples to that target slice |
| Validation | [`check_data_sample_ids_n()`](Documentation/Functions/check_data_sample_ids_n.qmd) checks whether enough samples remain |

All model matrices use a consistent sample-key convention derived from `dataset_name` and `age`.
This allows community, abiotic, coordinate, and spatial predictor matrices to be checked for identical row order before fitting.
:::

### 8. Model Input Preparation

The aligned data are converted into matrix form before fitting.
Community data are made wide and binarised, abiotic predictors are prepared and scaled, coordinates are projected, and spatial predictors are calculated.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segments**: `pipe_segment_model_input.R`, `pipe_segment_model_prepare_response.R`, `pipe_segment_model_spatial_shared.R`, `pipe_segment_model_spatial_samples.R`, and `pipe_segment_model_assemble.R`

| Target or function | Description |
|---|---|
| [`prepare_community_for_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/prepare_community_for_fit.qmd) | Converts long community records to a model matrix |
| [`binarize_community_data()`](Documentation/Functions/binarize_community_data.qmd) | Converts the response to binary presence/absence |
| [`prepare_abiotic_for_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/prepare_abiotic_for_fit.qmd) | Prepares abiotic predictors in wide form |
| [`scale_abiotic_for_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/scale_abiotic_for_fit.qmd) | Scales abiotic predictors and saves scaling attributes |
| [`prepare_coords_for_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/prepare_coords_for_fit.qmd) | Prepares coordinate tables for modelling |
| [`project_coords_to_metric()`](Documentation/Functions/project_coords_to_metric.qmd) | Projects coordinates to a metric coordinate reference system |
| [`compute_spatial_mev()`](Documentation/Functions/compute_spatial_mev.qmd) | Computes spatial Moran Eigenvector Maps |
| [`compute_spatiotemporal_mev()`](Documentation/Functions/compute_spatiotemporal_mev.qmd) | Computes spatiotemporal Moran Eigenvector Maps |
| [`prepare_spatial_predictors_for_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/prepare_spatial_predictors_for_fit.qmd) | Expands or aligns MEM predictors to model samples |
| [`scale_spatial_for_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/scale_spatial_for_fit.qmd) | Scales MEM predictors |
| [`assemble_data_to_fit()`](Documentation/Functions/assemble_data_to_fit.qmd) | Validates and assembles the final model input list |

For spatial analyses, MEMs are computed from geographic distances among sites.
For temporal analyses, MEMs can be computed from sample-level spatiotemporal coordinates so that broad temporal and spatial autocorrelation are handled together.
:::

### 9. Joint Species Distribution Model

The model is a Joint Species Distribution Model fitted with [{sjSDM}](https://theoreticalecology.github.io/s-jSDM/).
This framework fits all taxa jointly and estimates residual covariance among taxa after accounting for shared climate and spatial structure.
The residual covariance is the association signal of interest, but it is interpreted cautiously because unmeasured environment, dispersal, history, and true interactions can all contribute to it.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_model_fit.R`

| Target or function | Description |
|---|---|
| [`make_env_formula()`](Documentation/Functions/make_env_formula.qmd) | Builds the environmental model formula |
| [`fit_jsdm_model()`](Documentation/Functions/fit_jsdm_model.qmd) | Fits the `{sjSDM}` model |
| [`compute_jsdm_se()`](Documentation/Functions/compute_jsdm_se.qmd) | Computes post-hoc standard errors |
| [`evaluate_jsdm()`](Documentation/Functions/evaluate_jsdm.qmd) | Summarises model fit and species-level performance |
| [`check_convergence_jsdm()`](Documentation/Functions/check_convergence_jsdm.qmd) | Checks model convergence diagnostics |
| [`check_cuda_gpu_runtime()`](Documentation/Functions/check_cuda_gpu_runtime.qmd) | Checks GPU runtime availability |

**Model components**:

- **Environment**: non-redundant climate predictors and, where configured, interactions with age.
- **Space**: MEM predictors used as a spatial or spatiotemporal structure component.
- **Association**: residual low-rank covariance structure estimated by `{sjSDM}`.
- **Response**: binary presence/absence community matrix.

The project previously used HMSC-style MCMC modelling.
The workflow was migrated to `{sjSDM}` because gradient-descent fitting with optional GPU support is more practical for the large community matrices used here.
:::

### 10. Variance Decomposition

After fitting, the model is decomposed into abiotic, spatial, and association components.
This is the main comparative quantity used across spatial scale, time, data source, and biological resolution.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segment**: `pipe_segment_model_anova.R`

| Target or function | Description |
|---|---|
| [`get_anova()`](Documentation/Functions/get_anova.qmd) | Runs Monte Carlo ANOVA decomposition for the fitted model |
| [`extract_anova_fractions()`](Documentation/Functions/extract_anova_fractions.qmd) | Extracts named A, S, B, and shared fractions |
| [`aggregate_anova_components()`](Documentation/Functions/aggregate_anova_components.qmd) | Aggregates decomposition outputs across temporal branches |
| [`recalculate_anova_components()`](Documentation/Functions/recalculate_anova_components.qmd) | Converts raw fractions into comparable component summaries |

**Components**:

- **A**: variance associated with abiotic predictors.
- **S**: variance associated with spatial or spatiotemporal MEM predictors.
- **B**: residual association signal from the joint model covariance structure.
- **Shared fractions**: variance jointly attributable to combinations of A, S, and B.

Shared fractions are handled explicitly because climate, space, and association structure are not independent in ecological data.
:::

### 11. Temporal Network Analysis

Temporal analyses also calculate network metrics directly from the binarised community matrix.
These metrics provide a model-independent description of co-occurrence structure through time.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

**Pipe segments**: `pipe_segment_network_metrics.R` and `pipe_segment_network_summary_by_age.R`

| Target or function | Description |
|---|---|
| [`compute_network_metrics()`](Documentation/Functions/compute_network_metrics.qmd) | Calculates network-level metrics from a binary community matrix |
| [`plot_network_metrics_by_age()`](Documentation/Functions/plot_network_metrics_by_age.qmd) | Visualises temporal network trajectories |

**Metrics**:

- **Connectance**: proportion of realised links in the network.
- **Nestedness**: degree to which specialist taxa occur within subsets of broader assemblages.
- **Modularity**: degree to which the network is divided into sub-communities.
:::

### 12. Prediction and Visualisation

The fitted model stack is also used for prediction and exploratory visualisation.
These outputs are useful for checking model behaviour and communicating palaeoecological dynamics, but they remain preliminary while the analysis is being developed.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Technical details

Relevant helpers include [`build_prediction_frame()`](Documentation/Functions/build_prediction_frame.qmd), [`build_land_prediction_grid()`](Documentation/Functions/build_land_prediction_grid.qmd), [`predict_spatial_resolution_grid_age()`](Documentation/Functions/predict_spatial_resolution_grid_age.qmd), [`save_prediction_animation()`](Documentation/Functions/save_prediction_animation.qmd), and [`save_temporal_trajectory_animation()`](Documentation/Functions/save_temporal_trajectory_animation.qmd).

Summary and plotting helpers include [`summarise_spatial_variance_stack()`](Documentation/Functions/summarise_spatial_variance_stack.qmd), [`summarise_spatial_biotic_component()`](Documentation/Functions/summarise_spatial_biotic_component.qmd), [`plot_spatial_variance_stack()`](Documentation/Functions/plot_spatial_variance_stack.qmd), [`plot_spatial_biotic_component()`](Documentation/Functions/plot_spatial_biotic_component.qmd), and [`plot_anova_components_by_age()`](Documentation/Functions/plot_anova_components_by_age.qmd).
:::

## Active Pipeline Entry Points

The active pipeline scripts live in `R/Pipelines/`.
Reusable pipe segments live in `R/Pipelines/_pipes/`.
The store path is built from the active configuration's `target_store`, an optional spatial `store_suffix`, and the pipeline script name.

| Pipeline | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `pipeline_paleo_core.R` | Compact paleo development workflow |
| `pipeline_paleo_temporal.R` | Paleo temporal models across time slices |
| `pipeline_paleo_spatial_resolution.R` | Paleo spatial models across geographic units and biological resolutions |
| `pipeline_paleo_resolution_test.R` | Development check for paleo resolution routing |
| `pipeline_modern_spatial_resolution.R` | Modern spatial models across geographic units and biological resolutions |
| `pipeline_modern_spatial_resolution_test.R` | Development check for modern spatial-resolution routing |
| `pipeline_traits_reference.R` | Trait extraction, quality control, and functional-type reference generation |

## Current Configurations

The current configuration names are defined in `config.yml`.
They replace older names that were used before the paleo, modern, and traits workflows were separated.

| Configuration | Role |
|---|---|
| `project_cz_paleo` | Small paleo development workflow |
| `project_cz_modern` | Small modern development workflow |
| `project_traits_reference` | Trait processing and functional-type reference workflow |
| `project_paleo_temporal_europe` | Paleo temporal Europe |
| `project_paleo_temporal_america` | Paleo temporal North America |
| `project_paleo_temporal_asia` | Paleo temporal northern Asia |
| `project_paleo_spatial_continental` | Paleo spatial continental scale |
| `project_paleo_spatial_regional` | Paleo spatial regional scale |
| `project_paleo_spatial_local` | Paleo spatial local scale |
| `project_modern_spatial_continental` | Modern spatial continental scale |
| `project_modern_spatial_regional` | Modern spatial regional scale |
| `project_modern_spatial_local` | Modern spatial local scale |

## Current Pipeline Status

The live graphs below are generated by `targets::tar_visnetwork()` through the project progress helpers.
They show build state and dependencies rather than final scientific results.

### Paleo Temporal - Europe

<iframe
  src="/Documentation/Progress/paleo_temporal_europe/pipeline_paleo_temporal/project_status_small.html"
  width="100%"
  height="550px"
  style="border:none;">
</iframe>

### Paleo Spatial Resolution - Europe Continental

<iframe
  src="/Documentation/Progress/paleo_spatial_continental/europe/pipeline_paleo_spatial_resolution/project_status_small.html"
  width="100%"
  height="550px"
  style="border:none;">
</iframe>

### Modern Spatial Resolution - Europe Continental

<iframe
  src="/Documentation/Progress/modern_spatial_continental/europe/pipeline_modern_spatial_resolution/project_status_small.html"
  width="100%"
  height="550px"
  style="border:none;">
</iframe>

### Traits Reference

<iframe
  src="/Documentation/Progress/traits_reference/pipeline_traits_reference/project_status_small.html"
  width="100%"
  height="550px"
  style="border:none;">
</iframe>

## Current Limitations

The website intentionally exposes the method, code structure, progress graphs, function reference, and conference material while keeping the result interpretation provisional.
The model outputs, spatial comparisons, temporal trajectories, prediction experiments, and functional-type summaries are still being checked and should not be treated as final manuscript results.
